params.name: Mendelssohn's Baton
params.description:
On Feb 21, 1834 AD, Mendelssohn used a conducting baton for the first time in a performance.
In ancient Europe, people invented sign language as a method of command. As time passed, various methods of command such as tapping the floor with a cane also emerged. Finally, Mendelssohn, by chance, used a baton-shaped whalebone wrapped in leather and led a successful music performance as a conductor. The world's first conducting baton was born and gradually became a symbol of conductors.
